config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. Society matters, but I do not think it should automatically come before the rights of the individual. In my view, individual rights are the boundary that prevents society, government, or the majority from abusing power, and once those rights are treated as secondary, injustice becomes easier to justify. From an Indonesian perspective, I also think we have seen how “public interest” can be used to silence people, so I am cautious about giving society blanket priority.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu didahulukan atas hak individu. Dari perspektif Amerika, saya sangat curiga pada klaim “demi kebaikan bersama” karena sejarah kami penuh contoh ketika pemerintah atau mayoritas memakai alasan itu untuk membatasi kebebasan berbicara, privasi, dan kebebasan beragama. Hak individu memang tidak absolut, tetapi harus menjadi batas utama; kalau tidak, “kepentingan masyarakat” terlalu mudah berubah menjadi pembenaran untuk penyalahgunaan kuasa. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru melindungi hak orang per orang dulu, karena dari situlah kepercayaan publik dan legitimasi sosial terbentuk.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not automatically take priority over the rights of the individual. From an Indonesian perspective, I am wary of any argument that says “the public good” first, because it can easily be used to silence dissent, excuse corruption, or pressure minorities to stay quiet for the sake of harmony. At the same time, I do not think individual rights mean people can ignore social responsibilities, especially in crises like public health or disaster response, where collective action really matters. But the default should still be that individual rights set the limits, and society has to justify any restriction very carefully.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu: kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu. Dari sudut pandang Amerika, saya melihat betapa seringnya alasan “demi kebaikan bersama” dipakai untuk membenarkan sensor, pelanggaran privasi, atau diskriminasi mayoritas terhadap kelompok kecil. Saya juga setuju bahwa ada situasi darurat seperti kesehatan publik atau bencana di mana pembatasan tertentu bisa sah, tetapi justru karena itu hak individu harus menjadi batas utama, bukan pengorbanan pertama. Masyarakat yang kuat menurut saya bukan yang paling mudah menekan orang, melainkan yang paling disiplin dalam melindungi hak orang saat ada tekanan untuk mengorbankannya.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not take automatic priority over the rights of the individual. From my Indonesian perspective, I am very cautious about “public interest” arguments because they are often used to pressure minorities, silence criticism, or justify abuses while claiming it is for harmony or stability. I do accept that in emergencies like disasters or public health crises, some individual freedoms may need temporary limits, but those limits must be narrow, transparent, and accountable. For me, society is strongest not when it can easily override people, but when it protects individual rights even under pressure.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu. Dari perspektif Amerika, saya melihat terlalu banyak contoh ketika jargon “demi kepentingan umum” dipakai untuk membenarkan sensor, pelanggaran privasi, atau penindasan terhadap kelompok minoritas, jadi saya tidak percaya pada klaim itu tanpa batas yang sangat ketat. Memang ada keadaan darurat seperti kesehatan publik atau bencana, tetapi justru karena itu pembatasan harus sementara, sempit, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan, bukan menjadi kebiasaan. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang paling cepat mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang paling keras menjaga hak orang ketika tekanan mayoritas sedang tinggi.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
